Home / Services / Deck and Patio Construction

Deck and Patio Construction

Extend your living space outdoors with a custom deck or patio.

Enhance your outdoor living experience with a custom deck or patio from Consider It Done Construction Co. LLC. We design and build decks and patios throughout Baltimore, creating beautiful and functional outdoor spaces for relaxation and entertainment.

We understand the importance of choosing the right materials for Baltimore's climate. Our team will help you select durable and weather-resistant materials that will withstand the elements and provide years of enjoyment. From traditional wood decks to low-maintenance composite decks and paver patios, we offer a wide range of options to suit your style and budget.

We handle all aspects of the project, including design, permitting, construction, and finishing. Whether you envision a small deck for grilling or a large patio for entertaining, we'll work with you to create an outdoor space that meets your needs and enhances your property value.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Design Consultation – Discuss your vision, budget, and site conditions.
  2. 02 Step 2: Material Selection – Choose from a variety of decking, railing, and patio materials.
  3. 03 Step 3: Permitting and Preparation – Obtain necessary permits and prepare the site.
  4. 04 Step 4: Construction and Finishing – Build the deck or patio and add finishing touches.

Benefits

  • Increased outdoor living space
  • Enhanced property value
  • Improved curb appeal
  • Durable and weather-resistant construction

Frequently Asked Questions

What are the benefits of a composite deck compared to a wood deck?

Composite decks are low-maintenance, durable, and resistant to rot, insects, and fading. They also come in a variety of colors and styles.

Do I need a permit to build a deck or patio in Baltimore?

Yes, a permit is typically required to build a deck or patio in Baltimore. We handle the permit application process for you.

What type of patio pavers are best for Baltimore's climate?

We recommend pavers that are durable, weather-resistant, and slip-resistant. Concrete pavers, brick pavers, and natural stone pavers are all good options.

Get a Free Estimate
